items.author: Swyx
items.bio: Curator @aiDotEngineer, @latentspacepod co-host; organizer behind ai.engineer conferences
items.content_zh: Latent Space 联合主播、aiDotEngineer 策展人 Swyx 从今年大会排期里抛出一个耐人寻味的观察：Day 1 安排 "slop cannons"、Day 2 安排 "grown ups"，意外精准地还原了当下 AI engineering 最重要的分裂。他点名两场演讲作为两极代表——一场叫 "Slow Down"，一场叫 "Harness Engineering"，基本等于明牌告诉你这个行业正在分化：一边是狂奔出活的 vibe coder，一边是死磕 agent harness 严谨性的工程师。
items.content_en: Latent Space co-host and aiDotEngineer curator Swyx made a telling observation from this year's conference programming: scheduling the "slop cannons" on day one and the "grown ups" on day two turned out to be a pretty faithful representation of the biggest split in AI engineering right now. He pointed to two talks that capture both sides — a "Slow Down" talk and a "Harness Engineering" talk — essentially signaling that the field is bifurcating between fast-ship vibe-coders and engineers obsessed with rigorous agent harnesses.

items.author: Kevin Weil
items.bio: VP Science @OpenAI; former Pres @Planet, Head of Product @Instagram; LTC @USArmyReserve
items.content_zh: OpenAI 科学副总裁 Kevin Weil 发布 GPT-Rosalind——OpenAI 首个专门面向生物、药物发现和转化医学领域科研的 frontier model。该模型在化学、蛋白质工程、基因组学上做过专门训练，内置了科研人员日常用的数据库和工具知识。OpenAI 同时开放 request access 页面和 Life Sciences plugin，释放出的信号很明确：在 life sciences 这个领域，他们不打算靠通用模型凑合，要做领域专用的 frontier model。
items.content_en: OpenAI VP of Science Kevin Weil announced GPT-Rosalind, OpenAI's first frontier model built specifically for scientific research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. The model has been trained in chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics, with built-in knowledge of the databases and tools researchers actually use day-to-day. OpenAI is opening access via a request-access page and a new Life Sciences plugin, signaling a serious push into domain-specialized frontier models rather than leaving life sciences to general-purpose systems.

items.author: Kevin Weil
items.bio: VP Science @OpenAI; former Pres @Planet, Head of Product @Instagram; LTC @USArmyReserve
items.content_zh: OpenAI 科学副总裁 Kevin Weil 给新版 Codex computer use 下了个简短但很重的评价："shockingly good"。考虑到他是 OpenAI 内部人、大概率见过完整 roadmap，这种不打折扣的夸法值得留意——特别是 Codex 这波还同步加入了 in-app browser、computer use 和新 plugin。
items.content_en: OpenAI VP of Science Kevin Weil vouched for the new Codex computer use capability, calling it "shockingly good." Coming from someone inside OpenAI who presumably has access to the full internal roadmap, that kind of unqualified praise is worth noting — especially as Codex adds in-app browser, computer use, and plugins as part of the same push.

items.author: Peter Yang
items.bio: Product at Creator Economy; writes practical AI tutorials and interviews to 140K+ readers
items.content_zh: Creator Economy 产品负责人、AI 写作者 Peter Yang 点出了 Claude 和 Codex 应用的一个具体 UX 缺口：Web 和 mobile 上都没法方便地查看和编辑 markdown 文件。他的理由很直接——重度用户想亲手打磨最后 10% 的 instructions，而现在的 App 根本没给这个入口。一个来自日用者的小需求，但很说明问题。
items.content_en: Creator economy product lead and AI writer Peter Yang flagged a concrete UX gap in the Claude and Codex apps: there's no easy way to view and edit markdown files from web and mobile. His point is that power users want to hand-craft the last 10% of instructions, and the current apps don't let them. It's a small but telling feature request from someone who lives inside these tools daily.

items.author: Peter Yang
items.bio: Product at Creator Economy; writes practical AI tutorials and interviews to 140K+ readers
items.content_zh: Creator Economy 产品负责人 Peter Yang 分享了他现在做 skill 的固定套路：额外跑一个 eval agent，对第一个 agent 的输出做 yes/no 的二值检查，只要没全过就打回去让第一个 agent 继续干。他正在给 YouTube 缩略图和标题做一套这样的系统。可取走的点是：轻量级、二值评分的第二 agent 正在成为一线从业者做 skill 可靠性的标配。
items.content_en: Creator economy product lead Peter Yang shared a practical pattern he now uses every time he builds a skill: spin up a separate eval agent to do yes/no checks on the first agent's output, and if anything fails, kick the first agent back to keep working. He's currently building one for YouTube thumbnails and titles. The takeaway is that a lightweight, binary-graded second agent is becoming a default reliability layer for practitioners shipping real skills.

items.author: Nan Yu
items.content_zh: Linear 产品负责人 Nan Yu 对这一轮 AI agent 浪潮给了个冷冷的评价："就像当年 crypto 行业把所有既有金融概念重新发明了一遍，只不过这次快得多。恭喜我们自己。" 话不长但挺戳——agent 赛道正在用 AI 的外壳，把别的 stack 里早就存在的 workflow、角色、抽象再快速跑一遍。
items.content_en: Linear Head of Product Nan Yu made a dry observation about the current AI agent wave: "It's like how the crypto industry reinvented every existing financial industry concept, only this happens much faster. Congrats us." The quote is short but the subtext is sharp — agents are speed-running the same pattern of rediscovering workflows, roles, and abstractions that already exist in other stacks, just with AI branding slapped on.

items.author: Cat Wu
items.content_zh: Anthropic Claude Code + Cowork 负责人 Cat Wu 放出两个 Opus 4.7 实操建议。一，Claude Code 里 Opus 4.7 的默认 effort 已经拉到 xhigh，想调就用 /effort。二，她强烈建议团队把"怎么验证改动对不对"这件事明确告诉模型——把测试流程写进 claude.md，或者加一个 /verify-app skill——因为 Opus 4.7 的自我验证能力已经明显变强，你把那些本地 dev 小窍门喂给它，它会更好地兑现。
items.content_en: Anthropic's Cat Wu, who works on Claude Code and Cowork, shared two practical Opus 4.7 tips. First, Claude Code now defaults effort level to xhigh for Opus 4.7, adjustable via /effort. Second, she's urging teams to tell the model how to verify its own changes — put your testing workflow in claude.md, or add a /verify-app skill — because Opus 4.7 is meaningfully better at self-verifying and it rewards sharing local dev tips that are otherwise hard to discover.

items.author: Thariq
items.bio: Claude Code @Anthropic; prev YC W20, MIT Media Lab
items.content_zh: Anthropic Claude Code 成员 Thariq 一次性放出一组可用性升级，目标只有一个：让人真的把 Claude Code 用好。他们在文档里新增了一个 "What's New" 专区，把最重要的更新做成 digest；每月一次的 "what we shipped" webinar 已经开放报名；同时 /usage 正在迭代更新，本周还会有更多围绕"帮你看懂并优化自己用法"的动作放出。
items.content_en: Anthropic's Thariq, who works on Claude Code, rolled out a batch of usability improvements aimed at helping people actually get the most out of the product. They've added a curated "What's New" section in the docs that functions as a digest of the most important announcements, launched monthly "what we shipped" webinars that anyone can sign up for, and started rolling out updates to /usage — with more to come this week focused on helping users understand and optimize how they're using Claude Code.

items.author: Amjad Masad
items.content_zh: Replit CEO Amjad Masad 扔出一个很有杀伤力的价格点：用不到 10 美元，把你的 Web 应用变成一个 iOS 应用。作为 Replit 的一个工作流，这件事直接冲着一个长期门槛去——从发网站到发 App Store 之间那堵让独立开发者和小团队望而却步的成本和复杂度墙，被他按在地上摩擦。
items.content_en: Replit CEO Amjad Masad is pushing a striking new price point: turning your web app into an iOS app for less than $10. Packaged as a Replit workflow, the move is clearly aimed at flattening the cost and complexity gap between shipping a website and shipping to the App Store, a friction that has historically gated solo builders and small teams from native mobile distribution.

items.author: Amjad Masad
items.content_zh: Replit CEO Amjad Masad 同步放出两条平台层动作：一个 50% off 的促销，定位明确打在"跑 parallel agents、加速你的项目"这个用法上；以及一个新的 EU 部署区域。两件事合起来看，Replit 押的是 agent-native 的开发工作流 + 对企业友好的地理覆盖，而不是和别人卷模型层。
items.content_en: Replit CEO Amjad Masad also flagged two other platform moves: a 50% off promotion specifically framed as useful for running parallel agents to make faster progress on a project, and a new EU deployment region. Together these signal where Replit is pushing — agent-native development workflows and enterprise-friendly geographic footprint — rather than chasing model-layer differentiation.

items.author: Guillermo Rauch
items.content_zh: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 说 agent 和后端最难的那件事是 durability，他找了十年的独角兽是 @workflowsdk。逻辑很直白：你调的那个 LLM 一定会宕、那个服务一定会 rate limit 你、那个数据库一定会突然变慢、你一定会被 page。他想要 SQS / Kafka / microservices 组合在一起那种级别的可靠性，但死活不想背上那套运维成本——在他看来，workflow-sdk 就是这只独角兽终于出现的样子。
items.content_en: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch says the hardest thing about agents and backends is durability, and that @workflowsdk is what he's been looking for for a decade. His pitch: the LLM you're calling will go down, the service will rate limit you, the database will unexpectedly slow down, you will get paged. He wanted the reliability you get from combining SQS, Kafka, and microservices without actually signing up for that operational weight — and workflow-sdk is how he thinks that unicorn finally shows up.

items.author: Guillermo Rauch
items.content_zh: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 还抛出第二个判断：AI 的推进速度根本停不下来，你要做的事是用 Vercel 的 AI SDK 和 AI Gateway 去吃掉这种波动的 upside。他在贺 Anthropic 又出了一波好东西的同时也提醒：xAI、OpenAI、Google 都在路上。他的基本盘是——聪明的做法不是押一家 lab，而是在你产品和"当下赢的那家 lab"之间插一层抽象。
items.content_en: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch made a second point about the year ahead: the rate of progress in AI is relentless, so if you're building, you should capture the upside of that volatility via Vercel's AI SDK and AI Gateway. He congratulated Anthropic on another banger ship but flagged that xAI, OpenAI, and Google are all coming — his framing is that the smart move isn't to bet on one lab, it's to put an abstraction between your product and whichever lab is currently winning.

items.author: Alex Albert
items.content_zh: Anthropic 研究员 Alex Albert 列了他对新版 Opus 4.7 最喜欢的几点：异步任务和 follow instructions 都非常稳；effort level 的 token 控制更可预期，还新增了 xhigh 档；高分辨率图片不再被偷偷 downscale；做 UI、slides、docs 的"品味"明显更好了。最值得留意的是最后那条 "taste" ——等于默认承认以前的 Claude 输出"技术正确但审美平庸"，Anthropic 这波开始在这件事上正面下功夫。
items.content_en: Anthropic researcher Alex Albert shared his favorite things in the new Opus 4.7: very good at async work and following instructions, much more predictable effort levels for token control (with a new xhigh tier), no more downscaling of high-res images, and noticeably more taste in UIs, slides, and docs. The "taste" comment is the one to watch — it's a tacit admission that earlier Claude outputs were technically correct but aesthetically flat, and Anthropic is now shipping models that try to fix that.

items.author: Aaron Levie
items.content_zh: Box CEO Aaron Levie 的判断是：新版 Codex 代表 agent 之于知识工作者的又一次跃迁——能写代码、能用工具、能操作电脑的 agent，正在有能力在后台跑那些真正横跨一整条白领工作流的长任务。他给的例子很具体：写报告、给并购搭 data room、审合同、做客户 onboarding、生成营销素材、处理发票。这是一个很清楚的观点：下一波 agent 价值不在于替换某一个工具，而在于端到端跑完整条 workflow。
items.content_en: Box CEO Aaron Levie argues the new Codex is another jump in what agents look like for knowledge workers: agents that can code, work with tools, and use computers can now execute long-running tasks in the background across the full stack of white-collar work. His concrete examples — drafting reports, setting up data rooms for a merger, reviewing contracts, onboarding clients, generating marketing assets, processing invoices — are a clear thesis about where the next wave of agent value lands: not in replacing a single tool, but in running whole workflows end-to-end.

items.author: Ryo Lu
items.content_zh: Cursor 设计师、前 Notion / Stripe 的 Ryo Lu 在跟 @flowstated 一起做的新东西叫 "baby glass" ——一个在 Cursor 内部用的 prototyping 环境。它让设计师可以直接用 Cursor 3 新界面背后那套共享组件，在代码里快速构思、改、验证想法。这个方向值得留意：下一代设计工具不再是 Figma 那套静态 mockup，而是和生产应用共用真正组件库的 live-code sandbox。
items.content_en: Cursor designer Ryo Lu (formerly Notion and Stripe) previewed "baby glass," a new prototyping environment he's building with @flowstated at Cursor. It lets designers quickly imagine, remix, and test ideas in code using the same shared components powering Cursor 3's new interface. The framing is telling: the next generation of design tools isn't Figma-style mockups but live-code sandboxes that share the actual component library with the production app.

items.author: Ryo Lu
items.content_zh: Cursor 设计师 Ryo Lu 还晒了一下自己当下的写码 stack，全都在 Cursor 里跑：Opus 4.7 负责规划、Composer 2 负责构建和迭代、Codex / GPT-5.4 专门救场啃硬 bug。这是一个挺有参考价值的数据点——重度用户正按"场景"混搭多家厂商的模型：一个负责想、一个负责做、一个负责救火，而不是从头到尾押一个助手。
items.content_en: Cursor designer Ryo Lu also shared his current coding stack, all inside Cursor: Opus 4.7 for planning, Composer 2 for building and iterations, and Codex / GPT-5.4 for hard bugs. It's a useful data point on how power users are mixing model providers by job type — one model for thinking, one for doing, one for stuck — rather than defaulting to a single assistant across the board.

items.author: Garry Tan
items.bio: President & CEO @YCombinator; founder of GBrain / GStack / Garry's List
items.content_zh: YC 掌门 Garry Tan 这周给自己那套 AI 工具链集中推了一波更新：GBrain 的一批安全修复刚上线；/ship skill 做了一轮鲁棒性修复；还预告了 GBrain Voice ——起因是他和自己的 "claw" 找遍全网都没找到像样的 Gemini Live 端到端测试，就干脆自己写了一套，后续会开源发布。信号点不在具体功能，而在：一个 YC CEO 正在公开地自己建 agent 基建、自己吃自己的狗粮。
items.content_en: YC President & CEO Garry Tan shipped a round of updates on his personal AI stack this week: a batch of GBrain security fixes just dropped, the /ship skill got more robustness fixes, and he previewed GBrain Voice — built after he and his "claw" searched and couldn't find proper end-to-end Gemini Live tests, so they built their own. An open-source release is coming. The signal here is less the features themselves and more that a YC CEO is publicly building and eating his own dog food on agent infra.

items.author: Zara Zhang
items.content_zh: Builder Zara Zhang 把她眼里"最值得花时间的三件事"总结得很干脆：deep talk（和厉害的人做深入对话 / brainstorm）、deep read（扎实读一篇长内容）、deep play（随便挑点疯狂的点子和 AI 一起玩看会走到哪）。这是一份很紧凑的个人操作系统，刻意绕开了"执行型工作"，押的是能复利累积的"输入"。对任何日程正在被会议吞噬、偏离思考的人来说，是面不错的镜子。
items.content_en: Builder Zara Zhang distilled the three things she considers the highest-leverage use of her time: deep talk (brainstorming conversations with awesome people), deep read (consuming long-form content), and deep play (trying crazy random things with AI to see where they go). It's a compact personal operating framework that deliberately skips execution-heavy work in favor of inputs that compound — a useful mirror for anyone whose calendar is drifting toward meetings and away from thinking.

items.author: Zara Zhang
items.content_zh: Builder Zara Zhang 最近在反复押一个判断：HTML 之于 agent，就像 symphony 之于 Beethoven、play 之于 Shakespeare——天生的母语。她把自己 Frontend Slides 的 GitHub repo 做成了视频，还发布了 HTML Videos，结论一句话："HTML is eating everything"。更大的论点是——agent 根本不需要专门的 UI 或私有渲染器，用它们本来就从头到尾看得懂的那套 stack（HTML + 浏览器）足够吞下演示、视频等等一整片。
items.content_en: Builder Zara Zhang is doubling down on a thesis she's been pushing: HTML is to agents what symphony was to Beethoven or play was to Shakespeare — the native medium. She turned her Frontend Slides GitHub repo into a video and launched HTML Videos, arguing "HTML is eating everything." The broader claim is that agents don't need bespoke UIs or proprietary renderers; a stack they already understand end-to-end (HTML + browser) is good enough to swallow presentation, video, and more.

items.author: Peter Steinberger
items.bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather; came back from retirement to mess with AI
items.content_zh: 资深开发者 Peter Steinberger 正面回怼对 OpenClaw 的一个常见说法——一堆 GHSA（安全公告）堆着就是"项目太不安全"。他重新定义了这件事：OpenClaw 不是问题本身，而是"暴风雨将至"的指示器。换句话说，agent 时代的工具链正在把一类新的漏洞暴露出来，整个生态都会撞上，只不过先用的人先碰上。
items.content_en: Veteran developer Peter Steinberger pushed back on a common critique of OpenClaw — that the pile of GHSAs (security advisories) means the project is uniquely insecure. His reframe: OpenClaw isn't the problem, it's the indicator of the coming storm. In other words, agent-era tooling is surfacing a class of vulnerabilities that will show up across the ecosystem, and early adopters are just hitting them first.

items.author: Dan Shipper
items.content_zh: Every CEO Dan Shipper 做了一个 "philosopher draft"：让每家 frontier model 厂牌各"选"一位历史上的哲学家去雇佣，并解释为什么。这既像一个小游戏，又像一份罗夏墨迹测验——它逼你把 Anthropic、OpenAI、xAI、Google 各自的"思想家庭风格"讲清楚。顺便也提醒一件事：现在的 model lab 已经拥有足够鲜明的"人格"，可以被对标到西方哲学谱系里去。
items.content_en: Every CEO Dan Shipper ran a "philosopher draft" asking which philosopher from history each frontier model lab would hire and why. It lands somewhere between a parlor game and an actual Rorschach test for how you read Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, and Google: the exercise forces you to articulate what each lab's house intellectual style actually is. A nice reminder that model labs now have distinct personalities substantive enough to map onto the Western canon.

items.author: Sam Altman
items.content_zh: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman 一次性放出 Codex 的一堆重大升级。最大一条是 computer use——Altman 说"比我预期还实用"，它能在 Mac 上同时开起你的所有应用，并且不会打扰你正在手动做的事。Codex 还新增了 in-app browser 和大量新 plugin，并开始从使用中学习、主动建议它可以替你干什么。这些事合在一起讲一个故事：Codex 不再只是个 coding agent，它正在变成一个常驻在桌面上的"操作者"。
items.content_en: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced a large set of Codex upgrades. Computer use is the headline — Altman says it feels even more useful than he expected, and that it can drive all the apps on your Mac in parallel without interfering with what you're doing directly. Codex now also ships with an in-app browser, many new plugins, and has started learning from experience to proactively suggest things it can do for you. The unifying thesis: Codex is no longer just a coding agent, it's becoming an always-on desktop operator.

items.author: Claude
items.content_zh: Anthropic 官方账号 Claude 宣布 Claude Opus 4.7 今天在 Claude.ai、Claude Platform 和所有主流云平台同步上线。模型之外还有两类产品更新。Claude Code 里新增 /ultrareview 命令，会起一个专门的 review session 把你的改动全读一遍，照"严格 reviewer"的视角把问题拎出来；同时 auto mode 扩展给 Max 用户，长任务可以少被打断地跑。API 侧新增 xhigh effort level，位于 high 和 max 之间，给硬题目上的 reasoning 和延迟控制多留一档；Task budgets（beta）帮 Claude 在长任务里排优先级、管成本。
items.content_en: Anthropic's official Claude account announced Claude Opus 4.7 is available today on Claude.ai, the Claude Platform, and all major cloud platforms. Two product updates shipped alongside the model. In Claude Code, a new /ultrareview command runs a dedicated review session that reads through your changes and flags what a careful reviewer would catch, and auto mode has been extended to Max users so longer tasks run with fewer interruptions. On the API, a new xhigh effort level between high and max gives finer control over reasoning and latency on hard problems, and Task budgets (beta) help Claude prioritize work and manage costs across longer runs.

items.author: Anthropic Engineering
items.content_zh: Anthropic Engineering 官方博客：Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands。核心教训是——原来的设计把 Claude 的 harness、sandbox 和 session log 全塞进一个容器里，结果是：任何一次 failure 都会整条 session 挂掉，而对环境的假设也被硬编进了 harness。修法是把 "大脑"（Claude + harness）、"手"（sandbox 和工具）、"session"（可持久化的事件日志）三件事拆成各自独立的接口，各自可以独立失败、独立被替换。harness 从容器里搬出来，通过 execute(name, input) 调 sandbox，就像调普通工具一样；容器从此是 cattle 不是 pet。凭证也挪出 sandbox——Git token 在初始化时被写进本地 git remote，MCP 的 OAuth token 放在 vault 里、通过 proxy 代调，prompt injection 够不着。Session log 活在 Claude 的 context window 之外，用 getEvents() 可以随时查询，context 管理从此可逆。效果：p50 TTFT 降约 60%、p95 降超过 90%；并且"多大脑对多手"的架构不再需要每进一个新环境就重写一次 harness。
items.content_en: Anthropic Engineering: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands. The core lesson is that the old design put Claude's harness, sandbox, and session log in a single container — which meant any failure killed the whole session and assumptions about the environment got baked into the harness. The fix was to treat the "brain" (Claude + harness), the "hands" (sandboxes and tools), and the "session" (durable event log) as separate interfaces that can each fail or be replaced independently. The harness leaves the container and calls sandboxes via execute(name, input) like any other tool, so failed containers become cattle, not pets. Auth and credentials now live outside the sandbox — Git tokens are wired into the local git remote during setup, and MCP OAuth tokens sit in a vault behind a proxy, so a prompt injection can never reach them. The session log lives outside Claude's context window and can be interrogated with getEvents(), so context management becomes reversible. Results: p50 TTFT dropped roughly 60% and p95 dropped over 90%, and the system can now connect many brains to many hands without rewriting the harness for each new environment.

items.author: Claude Blog
items.content_zh: Claude 官方博客：Claude Managed Agents — 上生产快 10 倍。Anthropic 发布了 Claude Managed Agents：一套用来大规模构建和部署云端托管 agent 的可组合 API，今天在 Claude Platform 上进入 public beta。他们的定位很直接——过去要把一个 agent 推上生产，你得花几个月自己搭 sandbox 代码执行、checkpoint、凭证管理、scoped permission、端到端 tracing；现在这部分 Managed Agents 替你扛。你定义任务、工具和 guardrail，Anthropic 负责跑，内置的 orchestration harness 自己决定什么时候调工具、怎么管 context、怎么从错误里恢复。产品里包含：生产级 sandbox 和认证、断连也不丢状态的 long-running session、多 agent 协同（research preview）、scoped permission 以及集成进 Claude Console 的 execution tracing。内部在结构化文件生成上的测试显示，相比普通 prompt 循环，任务成功率最高提升 10 个百分点，越是硬题目收益越大。首批用户包括 Notion（在 Custom Agents 里派活给 Claude）、Rakuten（把企业 agent 接进 Slack 和 Teams）、Asana（在项目里驻场的 AI Teammates）、Vibecode（prompt 到部署好的 App）、Sentry（把 Seer 和一个写补丁的 Claude agent 配对）。
items.content_en: Claude Blog: Claude Managed Agents — get to production 10x faster. Anthropic launched Claude Managed Agents, a suite of composable APIs for building and deploying cloud-hosted agents at scale, available today in public beta on the Claude Platform. The pitch is that shipping a production agent used to require months of infrastructure work — sandboxed code execution, checkpointing, credential management, scoped permissions, end-to-end tracing — and Managed Agents now handles that for you. You define tasks, tools, and guardrails; Anthropic runs it on their infrastructure with a built-in orchestration harness that decides when to call tools, how to manage context, and how to recover from errors. Features include production-grade sandboxing and auth, long-running sessions that persist through disconnections, multi-agent coordination (research preview), and scoped permissions plus execution tracing built into the Claude Console. Internal testing on structured file generation shows up to 10 points better task success over a standard prompting loop, with the biggest gains on the hardest problems. Early customers include Notion (delegating work inside Custom Agents), Rakuten (enterprise agents plugged into Slack and Teams), Asana (AI Teammates inside projects), Vibecode (prompt-to-deployed-app), and Sentry (pairing Seer with a Claude patch-writer).

items.author: Latent Space
items.bio: Podcast by Swyx and Alessio covering AI engineering
items.content_zh: The Takeaway：做正经的 AI 产品意味着两年里把同一套系统重写五遍；赢家是那些把 CLI 放在核心、同时把 MCP 当作其上一层"窄且强权限"包装层的团队。Notion 的 Simon Last（AI 负责人）和 Sarah Sachs（AI 产品）做客 Swyx 和 Alessio 的节目，聊了他们为了在"企业系统级记录平台"里做 custom agents，一路上到底经历了什么。重写五次之后，他们现在的 stack 基于 100+ 工具，押的注很清楚：底层 agent 应该是一个 CLI，原生拥有文件系统访问和完整 compute 能力；MCP 坐在上面，作为更轻、更权限受控的接入面，去服务那些更窄的任务。Simon 的原话："总体来说我对 CLI 非常看好；MCP 在特定场景下我也仍然看好……MCP 本身就带一个很强的权限模型——你能做的事就是去调那些 tool。MCP 就是那种'笨但有效'的简单方案。" 反直觉的点在于：大多数团队默认二选一——要么全押 CLI 式的 coding agent，要么全押 MCP 原生的轻量 agent。Notion 的判断是两条都得有，因为同一个企业用户会在一个模型里既起草文档、又查数据库、又跑更深的工作流，你很难靠一个单一抽象解决。他们还抛出一个"软件工厂"式的观察：用户教育这件事已经翻过去了——和两三年前不一样，今天的客户一上来就懂 agent 能干什么；于是瓶颈从"讲清楚产品"转成了"把底下那套管道真正产品化"。
items.content_en: The Takeaway: building serious AI products means re-architecting the same system five times in two years, and the winners are the teams that keep CLIs at the core while treating MCP as a narrow, tightly permissioned wrapper around them. Notion's Simon Last (AI lead) and Sarah Sachs (AI product) talked with Swyx and Alessio about what it actually takes to ship "custom agents" inside a product used by enterprises as their system of record. Five rebuilds in, their stack now runs on 100+ tools and a deliberate bet: the underlying agent should be a CLI with first-class file system access and full compute, and MCP should sit on top as a lightweight, permissioned surface for narrow tasks. As Simon put it: "Broadly speaking, I'm really bullish on CLIs. I'm still bullish on MCPs in a certain environment… MCP inherently has a really strong permission model. Like, all you can do is call the tools. MCP is just, like, the dumb simple thing that works." The contrarian piece is that most teams assume you pick one — CLI-based coding agents vs. MCP-native lightweight agents. Notion's view is you need both, because enterprise users want the same model to draft a doc, query a database, and execute a deeper workflow, and you can't get that out of a single abstraction. They also made a "software factory" argument: user education has flipped, and unlike two or three years ago customers now immediately understand agent capabilities, which means the bottleneck has moved from explaining the product to productizing the plumbing underneath.
